reason: Response B has the balanced, clearly-structured argumentation I tend to produce: each paragraph advances a distinct, content-rich claim (definition, efficiency, physics, human meaning) with restrained first-person hedges and a tidy summative close. Response A reads like an imitator overshooting into "literary Claude" — relentlessly accumulative clauses, coined purple phrases ("small lunate tragedies of emptiness"), a repeated "which is to say" tic, and an incantatory ending that prioritizes mood over explanation. The disciplined richness of B fits my genuine register better than the ornate density of A.
